title: psoralea lanceolata subsp. scabra
intro: Psoralea lanceolata subsp. scabra, also known as the "coastal knawel", is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the knotweed family (Polygonaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including skin disorders, rheumatism, and infertility due to its ability to stimulate hormone production and improve blood circulation. The health benefits of Psoralea lanceolata subsp. scabra include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Psoralea lanceolata subsp. scabra is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
